description: Firmly fixed, supported, or balanced; regular, even, and continuous. 'Kolu' names the quality of constancy—the hand that doesn't shake, the pace that doesn't vary, the presence that remains reliable.
sound_symbolism: The word holds constant. 'Ko' begins with firm contact, stability established. Then 'lu' flows evenly, the same quality continuing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as adjective: 'kolu howeli' (steady wind). As verb: 'kolu' (to steady). Related to 'mureo' (stable) but emphasizes continuity and evenness rather than resistance to change.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Steady practice builds over time—not dramatic efforts but consistent, daily attention.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Steady hands and steady hearts—traditional crafts required the constancy that produces quality.
